zh.Kildonan, North Ayrshire, Scotland, Unit: 英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾多南
zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Montgomery於1510年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾多南。她於大約1530年與John Eccles在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1600年在她的家鄉享年90歲。

nameMeanings.name: Montgomery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Sainte-Foy-de-Montgomery and Saint-Germain-de-Montgomery (Calvados). In Ireland this surname was present in the medieval period, died out, and was then reintroduced from Scotland in the 17th century. It has been Gaelicized in Ireland as Mac Iomaire and in Scotland as Mac Gumaraid.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Montgomery was born in 1510, in Kildonan, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom. She married John Eccles about 1530, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1600, in her hometown, at the age of 90.

ko.Kildonan, North Ayrshire, Scotland, Unit: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬도난
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Montgomery(은)는 1510년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬도난에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1530년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 John Eccles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1600년 그녀의 고향에서 90세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Kildonan, North Ayrshire, Scotland, Unit: каилдонан, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Montgomery родилась в 1510 г. в местности каилдонан, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Eccles около 1530 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1600 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 90 лет.
